<!-- This file is generated by scripts/generate_challenge_docs.py. -->

# AVL easy: total forces

Wrap the `avl` executable, solve one tiny wing operating point, and retain a total-forces report.

## Why This Case Exists

The easiest AVL challenge should prove that a sparse, menu-driven legacy CLI can still do recognizable aerodynamic work through a deterministic batch session.

## Case Shape

- ID: `avl_easy`
- Family: `avl`
- Difficulty: `easy`
- Tier: `local_full`
- Style: `command`
- Workflow Slice: `aerodynamics`
- Target Kind: `command`
- Upstream Target: `avl`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/avl_easy/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

This case relies on its workflow without extra breadth assertions.

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the real-world example lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=avl_easy
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only avl_easy
```

## Prepared Inputs

- Render `challenges/cases/avl_easy/fixtures/minimal_wing.avl` to `{challenge_artifact_dir}/minimal_wing.avl` before the workflow runs.

## Fixtures

- `challenges/cases/avl_easy/fixtures/minimal_wing.avl`

## Workflow

### 1. Solve a tiny wing at one angle of attack and write total forces

Tool: `run_avl`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "cwd": "{challenge_artifact_dir}",
  "extra_argv": [
    "minimal_wing.avl"
  ],
  "stdin_text": "PLOP\nG\n\nOPER\nA A 4\nX\nFT\nforces.txt\n\nQUIT\n"
}
```

Expectations: json fields {'exit_code': 0}; files are non-empty ['{challenge_artifact_dir}/forces.txt'].

## What This Case Proves

- A minimal AVL wrapper can drive a real operating-point solve without relying on rich CLI help output.
- The challenge harness can retain a concrete aerodynamic forces report from a menu-driven solver.

## Known Limits

- This case focuses on one tiny geometry and one total-forces report rather than a broader AVL workflow.
- This case intentionally reports `skipped_unavailable` when `avl` is not installed.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: the shallowest useful AVL batch run.
# This keeps the first rung approachable while still producing a real aerodynamic report.
id = "avl_easy"
title = "AVL easy: total forces"
tier = "local_full"
style = "command"
slice = "aerodynamics"
family = "avl"
difficulty = "easy"

[example]
summary = "Wrap the `avl` executable, solve one tiny wing operating point, and retain a total-forces report."
motivation = "The easiest AVL challenge should prove that a sparse, menu-driven legacy CLI can still do recognizable aerodynamic work through a deterministic batch session."
proves = [
  "A minimal AVL wrapper can drive a real operating-point solve without relying on rich CLI help output.",
  "The challenge harness can retain a concrete aerodynamic forces report from a menu-driven solver.",
]
limitations = [
  "This case focuses on one tiny geometry and one total-forces report rather than a broader AVL workflow.",
  "This case intentionally reports `skipped_unavailable` when `avl` is not installed.",
]

[target]
kind = "command"
value = ["avl"]

[probe]
commands = [["avl"]]

[scaffold]
kind = "command"
function_name = "run_avl"
help_probe_args = ["-h"]

[setup]
[[setup.rendered_files]]
source = "fixtures/minimal_wing.avl"
destination = "{challenge_artifact_dir}/minimal_wing.avl"

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Solve a tiny wing at one angle of attack and write total forces"
tool = "run_avl"

[workflow.steps.arguments]
cwd = "{challenge_artifact_dir}"
extra_argv = ["minimal_wing.avl"]
stdin_text = """
PLOP
G

OPER
A A 4
X
FT
forces.txt

QUIT
"""

[workflow.steps.expect]
json_fields = { exit_code = 0 }
files_nonempty = ["{challenge_artifact_dir}/forces.txt"]
```
